reference: What brings you in here today? Um, I've just had difficulties with breathing for the past, I want to say eight, nine months. And um, my wife just told me to come in. It's just, she was worried about it and uh, I thought I'd just come in and have it checked out. Okay, so it's been going on for the last eight to nine months. Has it been getting worse at all? Yeah, I would think so. I would say so, yeah. So, Sorry, go on. So, it was just um, I feel like I just get short of breath more easily now. Okay, and is it short of breath with activities? Or are you getting short of breath with uh, sitting or resting as well? So when I'm, when I'm, with activity. With activity, okay. And the cough that you're getting, is it a wet cough or a dry cough? It's a dry cough. Okay. And are you bringing up any material at all, like any um, yellow or green sputum? No, no. Any blood? Um, no, no blood. No blood, okay. And with your shortness of breath, is it, are you having difficulty taking deep breaths, or do you find that you're taking more fast, shallow breaths? Um, just, I guess, just uh, shallow breaths. Okay, okay. And um, so this is over the last eight or nine months. Have you noticed any other symptoms at all, like chest pain, dizziness or fainting? No? No. Okay. And do your symptoms get, get worse at all when you're lying flat, like when you're sleeping? No. No. And do you ever wake up gasping for air? No. Okay. Um, and have you tried anything that's helped you uh, relieve some of these symptoms? Sorry, could you say that again? Uh, have you tried anything like over the counter, to help you relieve these symptoms? No, no I haven't. Um, have you traveled at all recently? No. Have you been in contact with anyone that's been sick? Um, no, not really. I, I did go out to the pharmacy to get my medications, but that's, that's the only time I actually interact with anyone outside of my home. And what, what medications do you take? Um, I just take aspirin. Aspirin, okay. Have you been diagnosed with any medical conditions? I had a heart attack. Oh okay, well when was that? Last year. Last year, well I'm so sorry and um, did they give you any medications to go on after that? Yeah yeah, yeah there, there were a couple of other medications as well. Something um, something, something -pril. And uh, I think there's uh, uh, rosuvastatin. Okay, and do you still take those? Yeah, I take those. Sometimes I forget that I do, I try to take them as much as I can. Okay, and you also take the aspirin. Alright, have you noticed any fevers or chills? No. Nausea or vomiting? Nope. And any chest pain? Uh, no. No, okay. Any increased fatigue? No. Any diarrhea or constipation? No. Any numbness or tingling anywhere? No. Any confusion or memory, like memory loss? Um, no, I don't think so. Any trouble urinating? Um, no. Okay, have you noticed any swelling um, in your feet, ankles, legs at all? No. Okay. Um, and how's your appetite been like? Um, it's been, it's been the same. Okay, and any um, significant weight changes? Have you lost any weight? No. No. Um, any exposure to fumes or dust within your environment? Um, not right now. When I was young, yeah. Okay, now did you, so did you have exposure to fumes or dust? Um, I used to work in a mine so. A mine? Yeah, lots of chemicals and things like that. Okay. And any exposure to any pets or animals? Uh, no. Besides your medical history of having a heart attack and some blood pressure, um, any other medical conditions? Uh, I just have high blood pressure. Okay, any allergies to any medications or any environmental allergies? No. Okay. Um, any hospitalizations besides when you had a heart attack, previously? No. Any surgeries? No. Okay. Did they tell you at all what the cause of your heart attack was? Um, they just uh, they said it was my, my diet. They said diet, okay. Yeah, something about cholesterol. Did you require any stents, or did you have to get a bypass surgery or anything like that? Uh, they put in, I think they put in stents. Stents, okay. Um, any family history of any heart or lung conditions? Yeah, um, my um, mom had heart failure. Okay, um, and how old was she when she had her heart failure? She was young, she was uh, 55. 55, okay. Um, and currently, right now, or sorry, do you have any family history of any cancers, any lung cancer or anything like that? No. And currently do you work or are you retired? I'm retired. Okay. And what's your living situation like right now? Do you live with anyone? I live with my wife. Okay, and do you smoke? Uh, no. Have you ever smoked? I did when I was young. How long would you say you smoked for? Um, I'd say 45 years. And how much would you smoke in an average day? Um, about a pack a day. Um, any recreational drugs like marijuana? No. And do you drink alcohol? Yeah I do. Um I'll, I'll drink a glass at night, every night. And a glass of, what would you drink? Oh just a, a glass of wine, white wine. Okay, so those are all the questions I have for you today. Any questions for me? No. Okay, so yeah, there's definitely like the coughing and shortness of breath are definitely concerning, and they've been going on for a pretty long time. Do you ever have any night sweats or anything like that? No. Okay. So there's a few things that we think about with the chronic shortness of breath and a cough. One could be just a COPD diagnosis with your long history of smoking. And this pulmonary function test, get an x-ray, chest x-ray as well. Um another thing that we need to look out for is uh, lung cancer. And since you have been smoking for a pretty long time, even though you may have quit, which is really great, but that long history of smoking, especially also with um, you working in a mine, those are some things that means we can test as well, and so we may require like a chest x-ray, and even um, a CT scan of your lungs as well. Um, so yeah, first we'll just do a couple tests, do a physical examination, get your vitals, do some blood work and do some of those tests and then we'll also get a COVID swab test done for you as well, just in case. Does that sound good? Yeah. Alright, so we'll be with you shortly.
